notes.text:
An update is available for 389-ds-base.
This update affects Rocky Linux 10.
A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE list
notes.title: Topic

notes.text:
389 Directory Server is an LDAP version 3 (LDAPv3) compliant server. The base packages include the Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) server and command-line utilities for server administration.

Security Fix(es):

* 389-ds-base: 389-ds-base: Heap buffer overflow in sasl_io_recv() via padded SASL UNBIND (CVE-2026-11610)

* 389-ds-base: 389-ds-base: integer overflow in SASL packet length bypasses size limit leading to heap buffer overflow (CVE-2026-11774)

For more details about the security issue(s), including the impact, a CVSS score, acknowledgments, and other related information, refer to the CVE page(s) listed in the References section.

This advisory is provided by Ctrl IQ, Inc for Rocky Linux systems. For more information about CIQ security practices, visit https://ciq.com/security.
